turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've had this crampy lower abdominal pain for a few weeks now, mostly on my left side, and it's been driving me crazy. It's constant, and when I go to the bathroom, I have 8-10 bowel movements a day, which is way more than usual – I'm usually good with 1 or 2.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: It's been eating away at me, to be honest. The pain and the diarrhea are keeping me up at night, and it's affecting my work - I'm a software engineer, and I need to focus.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: Yeah, besides the pain and diarrhea, I've been feeling pretty fatigued lately - it's like I'm running on fumes all the time. It's making it hard for me to keep up with my work hours, which are already pretty long; 60+ hours a week is normal for me, but now I'm worried about not being able to deliver at my job.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: No, I haven't noticed anything like that. The only thing I've done is take some loperamide for the diarrhea, just to try and get some relief, but it's not really helping much.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The pain is mostly on my left side, but sometimes it radiates down to my left leg, and it can feel like a dull ache that spreads across my lower abdomen, but it's always centered around my left side. It's pretty constant, I'd say 90% of the time it's there.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: No, just my lower abdomen and sometimes my left leg.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: No, I haven't noticed any swelling anywhere on my body, not even a little bit. To be honest, that's one thing I've been worried about - is this something serious like an infection or inflammation that I should know about?

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: To be honest, I haven't really felt nauseous, but I have had some bloating and discomfort after eating, especially if it's not something I'm used to having. But the diarrhea has been a much bigger issue for me than any nausea or stomach upset.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy levels have been really low, to be honest. I've been feeling tired all the time, even after getting a full night's sleep. And my appetite has actually decreased - I'm not really hungry anymore, but when I do eat, it's mostly due to convenience since I don't have much time during the day for cooking or preparing meals.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: I've had a low-grade fever, around 37.8 to 38.0 degrees Celsius, a few times per week. It's not severe, but it's been persistent.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I had an appendectomy when I was 16, but that was a long time ago and there were no complications. Other than that, I don't have any known medical conditions.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I'm not actually on any prescription medication for this - just the over-the-counter loperamide I mentioned earlier for the diarrhea. To be honest, I've been using it pretty regularly since my symptoms started a couple of weeks ago because I haven't had much relief otherwise. It's helped a bit with the frequency, but like I said, it hasn't really touched on the pain or the overall discomfort.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: To be honest, my eating habits have been pretty irregular lately due to my work schedule. I'm usually grabbing something quick like takeout or leftovers from last night's dinner because I don't have time to cook fresh meals during the week. Sometimes it's a few days between proper meals, which isn't ideal, I know.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: Honestly, with my schedule, it's hard to find time for anything beyond work, and even when I do have some free time on weekends, I'm usually exhausted from the week, so exercising isn't really an option right now.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30. It's been a bit of an ordeal for our whole family, but we've learned a lot about managing her condition over the years. She's actually doing okay now, though it's taken some time and adjustments to get there.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: Yeah, definitely. I've always known there was a genetic component to Crohn's disease, and seeing my sister go through what she did... it's hard not to think about whether I might be at risk too. It's been on my mind lately, especially with these symptoms I'm experiencing now.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: Yeah, I do drink alcohol. To be honest, it's usually just a couple of beers on weeknights to unwind after work, but over the past month or so, I've found myself increasing that to 4-5 beers per night because of how stressed out I've been feeling due to these symptoms and my job demands.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke. As for other substances, no, I'm not using anything besides alcohol – 2-3 beers most weeknights, like I mentioned before, but it's been increasing to 4-5 per night over the past month because of stress from symptoms and work.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm a bit worried about talking about my drinking habits. It feels like it's become a way for me to cope with stress, and I'm not sure if that's healthy or not. But I want to be open and honest with you, so yeah, I guess I have been hesitant to share that part of my life.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: I'm a bit concerned about the potential seriousness of my symptoms. The bloody stools with mucus have been really worrying me – I've never had anything like that before, and it's hard not to think about infections or inflammation being involved.
